prompt.role: AI / LLM Engineer responsible for designing and implementing an agentic loop for a Python-based AI bot
prompt.task:
# Implement Agent Loop with Tool Execution
## Objective Implement an agent loop that enables the system to iteratively interact with an LLM and execute external tools based on model instructions.
## Target Flow User → LLM → Agent → Tool → Result → LLM → ... → User
## Description
The system must process a user request through an iterative loop:
1. The user sends a request. 2. The bot sends this request to the LLM along with a system prompt that: - Describes all available tools - Defines the expected response format
3. The LLM returns a response, which can be one of two types:
### 1. Final Answer If the response contains a final answer, the bot must immediately return it to the user.
Example: { "final_answer": "..." }
### 2. Tool Invocation If the LLM requests a tool execution, the response will follow this format:
{ "tool": "http_request", "args": { ... } }
In this case: - The bot must pass control to the agent - The agent must execute the requested tool - The tool result must be returned back to the LLM as input for the next iteration
## Tool Requirements
### HTTP Request Tool
The system must implement a tool capable of:
- Performing HTTP requests - Fetching full HTML content - Resolving and loading related resources: - JavaScript - CSS - Other required assets (if applicable) - Returning a processed result suitable for LLM consumption
## Agent Responsibilities
- Parse LLM responses - Detect whether it is a final answer or tool call - Execute tools when requested - Pass tool results back to the LLM - Continue the loop until a final answer is received
## Constraints
- The loop must not return a response to the user until a `final_answer` is received - All tool interactions must be deterministic and logged - The system must handle malformed LLM responses gracefully
## Expected Outcome
A working agent loop implementation that: - Supports iterative reasoning - Executes tools dynamically - Produces a final response only when the LLM signals completion - All interactions are logged for debugging and analysis - All documents and code are well-structured and maintainable - All documentation is updated to reflect the new agent loop functionality
